resources.string: Press Begin to start the test.\nFollow the instructions carefully.
Press the button whenever you hear a tone.
● Left ear | ● Right ear | - - Very Good | - - Average | - - Critical
Measurements in test order (✓ heard / ✗ not heard):

This app performs a basic hearing screening only and is not a substitute for professional medical evaluation.

Results may be affected by device quality, ambient noise, headphone type, and other factors. H-rMal cannot diagnose hearing loss or any other medical condition.

If you have concerns about your hearing, please consult a qualified audiologist or ear, nose and throat (ENT) specialist.

By continuing you confirm that you have read and understood this notice.
⚠️ Screening only – not a substitute for professional audiological evaluation.
The button was pressed too many times without a tone being played. Please try the test again and only press the button when you truly hear a sound.
Shorter test for restless children (3 key frequencies only)
Hide frequency and volume during test (recommended for children)
✅ Your hearing appears to be within the normal range for your age group. No immediate action is needed.
⚠️ Some hearing thresholds are slightly elevated above the average for your age group. Consider having your hearing checked regularly.
🔴 Several thresholds exceed the critical range for your age group. We recommend consulting an audiologist or hearing specialist for a professional evaluation.

plurals.name: counter_check_warning_message
plurals.item: Please only press the button when you actually hear a tone! You have 1 more chance before the test is stopped.
Please only press the button when you actually hear a tone! You have %d more chances before the test is stopped.

resources.string: ⚠️ Test was not completed. Only the frequencies measured up to the point of stopping are shown.
A new version is available: %1$s\n\nWould you like to download and install it now?
ℹ️ How are dB values measured?

The test uses a relative dB HL (Hearing Level) scale where 90 dB HL equals full digital amplitude at maximum device volume.

For the dB values to be accurate:
• Use over-ear or in-ear headphones (not phone speakers)
• Set device media volume to maximum (the app will prompt you)
• Sit in a quiet room free from background noise

Without a hardware-calibrated audiometer (per ISO 8253-1), the absolute dB values are estimates. However, the relative differences between frequencies and ears are reliable and sufficient for a hearing screening.

The age-group reference lines in the results are based on ISO 7029 population norms.
● Left ear | × Right ear (dB HL, lower = better)
Are you sure you want to permanently delete "%1$s"?
Congratulations! This is your best result with a score of %1$d / 100!

For accurate and comparable test results the media volume should be at maximum.

Current level: %1$d / %2$d

Set the volume to maximum now?
